claim_actionable.description:
Atomically claim an open actionable for an agent.

Uses Redis WATCH/MULTI to ensure exactly one agent wins the race.
Returns a result dict indicating success or the reason for failure.

Args:
    actionable_id: The actionable ID to claim (e.g., 'act-a1b2c3d4')
    agent_id: The agent ID claiming this actionable (e.g., 'eng1')
    r: Optional Redis client (uses shared connection if not provided)

release_actionable.description:
Release a previously claimed actionable.

Only the agent that claimed the actionable can release it.
Supports releasing to 'done', 'cancelled', or 'open' (unclaim).

Args:
    actionable_id: The actionable ID to release
    agent_id: The agent ID releasing this actionable (must match claimed agent)
    new_status: Target status — 'done', 'cancelled', or 'open' (unclaim)
    r: Optional Redis client (uses shared connection if not provided)

get_slot_availability.description:
Scan Google Calendar and return free time slots as plain text.

Checks each weekday (Mon-Fri) in the specified range for availability
within the working window. Returns one line per day with available slots.

Output format example:
    Mon, Mar 2: 9-10.30am, 11.15am-12pm, or 1-7pm
    Tue, Mar 3: 9am-12pm or 2.30-7pm
    Wed, Mar 4: fully booked
    Thu, Mar 5: 9-7pm (free all day)

IMPORTANT: Prefer omitting start_date to let the tool auto-detect the
next working day. Only pass start_date if the user specifies an exact date.
When the user says a day name like "monday", do NOT guess the date —
omit start_date and the tool will find the correct next Monday automatically.

Args:
    days: Number of weekdays to scan (default 5, i.e. one work week).
    start_date: Start date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Defaults to today
        (or next Monday if today is a weekend).
        IMPORTANT: prefer omitting this — the tool auto-detects the next weekday.
    window_start: Working window start time in HH:MM 24h format (default "09:00").
    window_end: Working window end time in HH:MM 24h format (default "19:00").
    account: Google account email. Defaults to primary account.
    calendar_id: Calendar ID to scan (default "primary").

get_system_pulse.description:
Get system health pulse score and breakdown (VT0).

Returns score (0-100), status (green/yellow/red),
and per-component breakdown including impeded AOW count.

get_action_queue.description:
Get pending HITL items (action queue) (VT0).

Args:
    domain: Optional domain filter. Empty string for all.

Returns:
    Dict with pending items and decision debt.

set_agent_permission.description:
VT4: Set agent permission (owner-only).

Args:
    agent_id: The agent to update.
    permission: One of: execute, propose, advise, paused.

Returns:
    Success status.

set_agent_goals.description:
VT2: Update agent's active goals.

Args:
    agent_id: The agent to update.
    goals: JSON string of goals list.

Returns:
    Success status.

add_domain_channel.description:
VT2: Add a domain channel.

Args:
    domain: Domain name (e.g., "health", "learning").
    channel_id: Slack channel ID.

Returns:
    Success status.

set_escalation_channels.description:
VT2: Configure escalation cascade order.

Args:
    channels: Comma-separated channel list (e.g., "slack,telegram,email").

Returns:
    Success status.

emit_custom_event.description:
Emit a custom event to the CARLOS-OS event bus.

Args:
    event_type: Event type (e.g., 'actionable.created', 'decision.logged')
    policy_class: Dispatch policy (immediate/scheduled/windowed/batched/reactive/advisory/proactive)
    summary: Human-readable event summary
    domain: Event domain (e.g., 'career', 'finance', 'general')
    details: Optional payload details dict
    preferred_channel: Channel preference (slack/email/calendar/any)
    vt_tier: VT governance tier (0-4)
    correlation_id: Optional correlation ID for event chains

query_events.description:
Query recent events from the event bus.

Args:
    stream: Specific stream name. If omitted, queries all 10 streams.
    event_type: Optional filter by event type.
    count: Maximum number of events to return (default 20).

event_history.description:
Query event history with optional filters.

Args:
    stream_category: Stream category to query (deadline/aow/commitment/
                     ask/actionable/decision/agent/system/all).
    event_type: Optional exact event type filter.
    source_agent: Optional filter by source agent name.
    domain: Optional filter by event domain.
    count: Maximum number of events to return (default 20).

get_event_metrics.description: Get event counts by stream category, plus total, dead_letter, and audit.

trim_event_streams.description:
Trim all event streams to a maximum length.

Args:
    maxlen: Maximum entries per stream (default from CARLOS_STREAM_MAXLEN).

federation_upsert_org_registry.description: Upsert one organization record in federation org_registry.

federation_upsert_capability_registry.description: Upsert one capability record in federation capability_registry.

federation_append_settlement_ledger.description: Append one entry to federation settlement_ledger stream.

federation_list_org_registry.description: List federation org IDs from registry index.

federation_list_capability_registry.description: List capability IDs declared by one org.

governance_check.description:
Run a VT governance pre-check on an action before executing it.

Validates whether the action is allowed under VT tier governance rules.
Use this before performing sensitive operations to verify permission.

Args:
    action: The action to check (e.g., 'deploy', 'store_fact')
    agent_name: The agent requesting the action
    vt_tier: VT risk tier (0=auto, 1=log, 2=approve, 3=advise, 4=block)
    payload: Optional action payload for size/content checks

Returns:
    JSON with {allowed, reason, tier_policy, metadata}

classify_action.description:
Classify an action and discover applicable governance standards.

Looks up which governance standards apply to a given action.

Args:
    action: The action to classify (e.g., 'store_fact', 'deploy', 'pr_merge')

Returns:
    JSON with {standards: list[dict], action_classified: bool}

compliance_check.description:
Validate that an action's output meets governance compliance standards.

Args:
    action: The action being validated
    agent_name: The agent that performed the action
    vt_tier: VT risk tier of the action
    payload: The action's output payload to validate

Returns:
    JSON with {compliant: bool, violations: list[str]}

kb_get_entity.description:
Fetch a single KB entity by ID.

Args:
    entity_id: The entity identifier (e.g. ``person:alice``).

Returns:
    JSON string with entity data, or a descriptive message if not found.

kb_search_entities.description:
Search KB entities by text or alias (case-insensitive substring match).

Args:
    query: Search string — matched against ``text`` and ``aliases``.
    entity_type: Optional filter (e.g. ``PERSON``, ``ORG``).
    limit: Max results (default 20).

Returns:
    JSON array of matching entities, or a descriptive message if none found.

kb_list_entities_by_type.description:
List KB entities of a given type, ordered by recency.

Args:
    entity_type: One of PERSON, ORG, LOCATION, PROJECT, TECHNOLOGY,
                 FINANCIAL_AMOUNT, DATE.
    limit: Max results (default 50).

Returns:
    JSON array of entities, or a descriptive message if none found.

kb_get_entity_relations.description:
Get directed relations for an entity.

Args:
    entity_id: Source entity ID.
    direction: ``out`` (entity is subject), ``in`` (entity is object),
               or ``both`` (default).
    limit: Max relations to return.

Returns:
    JSON array of relations, or a descriptive message if none found.

kb_get_entity_temporal.description:
Get a temporal profile for an entity (first/last seen, doc spread).

Derives the temporal profile from the entity's source documents and
metadata timestamps. Falls back to entity ``updated_at`` if no docs.

Args:
    entity_id: Entity ID to profile.

Returns:
    JSON string with temporal profile, or a descriptive message if not found.

kb_hybrid_search.description:
Search KB entities using hybrid BM25 + vector + graph fusion.

Args:
    query: Free-text search query.
    entity_types: Comma-separated entity type filter (e.g. ``PERSON,ORG``).
                  Empty string means all types.
    limit: Max results to return (default 10).

Returns:
    JSON array of results sorted by RRF score, or a descriptive message.

kb_get_community.description:
Get the community assignment for an entity.

Community analysis writes `community_id`, `degree_centrality`, and
`betweenness_centrality` directly onto the entity HASH plus aggregate
member counts into ``carlos:kb:communities``.

Args:
    entity_id: Entity ID to look up.

Returns:
    JSON object with community info, or a descriptive message if not found.

kb_get_top_ranked.description:
Get the top-ranked KB entities from the global ranking ZSET.

Reads from ``carlos:kb:ranking`` sorted set (score = relevance rank,
higher = more relevant).

Args:
    limit: Max entities to return (default 20).

Returns:
    JSON array of (entity_id, score) pairs, or a descriptive message.

kb_get_entity_neighbors.description:
Get graph neighbours of an entity up to N hops (max 2).

Args:
    entity_id: Seed entity ID.
    hops: Number of hops (1 or 2; capped at 2).
    limit: Max neighbours to return per hop.

Returns:
    JSON object with neighbours per hop, or a descriptive message.

kb_cross_correlations.description:
Get cross-correlated entities (Jaccard co-occurrence scores).

Reads from ``carlos:kb:cooccur:{entity_id}`` ZSET written by
``carlos_os.retrieval.correlate.cooccurrence()``.

Args:
    entity_id: Source entity ID.
    limit: Max correlated entities to return.

Returns:
    JSON array of correlated entities with Jaccard scores, or a message.

kb_analytics_summary.description:
Return a summary of KB analytics.

Includes entity counts by type, total entity count, community count,
and top-5 ranked entities.

Returns:
    JSON object with summary statistics.

search_episodes.description:
Search episode summaries from session history.

Episodes are narrative summaries of significant work sessions — what happened,
who was involved, and what the outcome was.

Args:
    query: Search string (case-insensitive match in title, summary, tags, outcome)
    domain: Optional — filter by tag matching this domain
    agent: Optional — filter to episodes involving this agent
    limit: Max results (default 20)

search_decisions.description:
Search architectural and process decisions with rationale.

Decisions record what was chosen, why, and what alternatives were considered.
Use this to check for precedent before making similar choices.

Args:
    query: Search string (case-insensitive match in title, decision, rationale)
    status: Optional — filter by status ('accepted', 'superseded', 'rejected')
    limit: Max results (default 20)

search_patterns.description:
Search recurring patterns and anti-patterns from codebase history.

Patterns capture how work actually gets done — validated approaches,
their context, examples, and known anti-patterns.

Args:
    query: Search string (case-insensitive match in name, description, context, examples)
    limit: Max results (default 20)

get_agent_memories.description:
Get memories for a specific agent (bounded to prevent token overflow).

Agent memories capture capabilities, lessons learned, roles, and preferences
that persist across sprints. Use during preflight to load agent context.

Args:
    agent_id: Agent identifier (e.g., 'orchestrator', 'eng1', 'qa', 'arch', 'sec', 'eng2', 'pm-a')
    memory_type: Optional filter — 'capability', 'lesson', 'role', 'preference'
    limit: Maximum results to return (default 30, max 50)

search_error_logs.description:
Search error logs with resolutions from past sessions.

Error logs capture what went wrong, the error type, and how it was resolved.
Use when encountering errors to find past resolutions.

Args:
    query: Search string (case-insensitive match in description, resolution, tags)
    error_type: Optional filter — 'integration', 'config', 'runtime', 'permission', etc.
    limit: Max results (default 20)

knowledge_stats.description:
Get overview statistics of the knowledge base.

Returns counts of all entity types, domain coverage, and agent memory
distribution. Useful for understanding what knowledge is available.

store_fact.description:
Store or update a fact in memory.

Args:
    domain: Knowledge domain — use canonical domains: engineering,
        architecture, infrastructure, security, sprint-history, workflow,
        backlog, code-review, agents, governance, personal, career, work,
        finance, ip, korza.
    key: Fact identifier within the domain
    value: The fact content
    confidence: Confidence score 0.0-1.0
    source: Where this fact came from
    metadata: Optional additional metadata

retrieve_fact.description:
Retrieve a specific fact by domain and key.

Args:
    domain: Knowledge domain
    key: Fact identifier

list_facts.description:
List facts, optionally filtered by domain. Returns up to `limit` results.

Use search_facts() for finding specific facts — this tool is for browsing.

Args:
    domain: Filter to this domain only. If omitted, returns across all domains.
    limit: Maximum results to return (default 20, max 50).

delete_fact.description:
Delete a fact from memory.

Args:
    domain: Knowledge domain
    key: Fact identifier

search_facts.description:
Search facts using BM25 full-text search with optional filters and re-ranking.

Provide a specific, descriptive search query for best results. The search
uses BM25 scoring (term frequency / inverse document frequency) — more
specific queries yield more relevant results than broad ones.

Args:
    query: Search query (BM25 full-text search). Be specific.
    domain: Optional domain filter — canonical domains: engineering,
        architecture, infrastructure, security, sprint-history, workflow,
        backlog, code-review, agents, governance, personal, career, work,
        finance, ip, korza. Use list_domains() to see all with counts.
    source: Optional source filter (e.g., 'arch_agent', 'etl:b9c33db6').
    tags: Optional tag filter — comma-separated (e.g., 'actionable,decision').
    limit: Maximum results to return (default 10, max 50).
    use_rerank: If True, apply Voyage AI re-ranking for higher precision.

create_note.description:
Create a new note and store it in Redis.

Args:
    title: Note title (short, descriptive).
    content: Note body text (can be multi-line).
    domain: Knowledge domain (e.g., engineering, personal, finance).
    source: Origin — manual, slack, meeting, or agent.
    tags: Comma-separated tags (e.g., "idea,urgent,project-x").
    agent_id: Which agent created the note (empty for manual).
    source_ref: External reference (Slack thread_ts, meeting ID).

Returns:
    JSON with the created note ID.

search_notes_tool.description:
Search notes by substring match in title and content.

Args:
    query: Search text (case-insensitive substring).
    domain: Optional domain filter.
    limit: Max results (default 20).

Returns:
    JSON array of matching notes.

get_note_tool.description:
Retrieve a single note by ID.

Args:
    note_id: The note identifier (e.g., note-a1b2c3d4).

Returns:
    JSON with full note data, or error if not found.

archive_note_tool.description:
Archive a note (soft delete).

Args:
    note_id: The note identifier to archive.

Returns:
    JSON with success status.

process_note_tool.description:
Process a note with LLM: correct typos, suggest tags, summarize.

Args:
    note_id: The note identifier to process.

Returns:
    JSON with processing results (corrected title, tags, summary).

ingest_meeting_transcript_tool.description:
Ingest a meeting transcript as a Smart Note with structured extraction.

Args:
    transcript: Full meeting transcript text.
    title: Optional title override (LLM generates one if omitted).
    domain: Knowledge domain (e.g., engineering, personal).
    source_ref: External reference (meeting ID, Drive file ID).

Returns:
    JSON with note_id, title, participants, decisions, action_items.

extract_actionables_tool.description:
Extract actionable items from a note and create them automatically.

Args:
    note_id: The note identifier to extract actionables from.

Returns:
    JSON with note_id and list of created actionable IDs.

list_situations.description:
List situations filtered by state, domain, and urgency threshold.

Args:
    state: Filter by lifecycle state — emerging, active, evolving, resolved,
           dissolved. If omitted, returns all non-terminal (non-resolved/
           non-dissolved) situations.
    domain: Filter by domain (e.g. 'career', 'finance', 'family', 'general').
    min_urgency: Minimum urgency threshold (0.0–1.0, default 0.0).
    limit: Max results (default 50).

Returns:
    JSON array of situations sorted by urgency descending, or a descriptive
    message if none found.

get_situation.description:
Get full situation detail including events, trajectory, and watchers.

Args:
    situation_id: The situation ID (e.g. 'sit-abc12345').

Returns:
    JSON string with full situation data, lineage events, trajectory
    snapshots, and associated watchers. Returns a descriptive message
    if the situation is not found.

get_situation_trajectory.description:
Get confidence/urgency time series for a situation.

Args:
    situation_id: The situation ID.
    since_hours: How many hours back to look (default 168 = 7 days).

Returns:
    JSON object with situation_id and array of trajectory point snapshots,
    sorted by timestamp ascending. Returns a descriptive message if no
    data is found within the time window.

search_situations.description:
Free-text search across situation names and descriptions.

Args:
    query: Search string — case-insensitive substring match on name
           and description.
    limit: Max results (default 20).

Returns:
    JSON array of matching situations with summary fields, or a
    descriptive message if none match.

list_watchers.description:
List situation watchers.

Args:
    active_only: If True (default), return only watchers in the active set.
                 If False, scan all watcher keys.

Returns:
    JSON array of watcher summaries, or a descriptive message if none found.

create_situation.description:
VT1: Create a new situation from user input.

Args:
    name: Situation name — short, descriptive, identifies the hypothesis.
    description: What is happening and why it matters.
    domain: Domain context (default 'general'). E.g. 'career', 'finance',
            'family', 'health', 'korza'.
    confidence: Initial confidence score (0.0–1.0, default 0.5).
    urgency: Initial urgency score (0.0–1.0, default 0.5).

Returns:
    JSON string with the created situation summary, or an error message.

create_actionable.description:
Create a new actionable (FUP, commitment, promise, deadline, task).

Args:
    title: Short description of the action needed
    type: One of: fup, commitment, promise, deadline, task, decision, escalation
    description: Detailed description
    domain: Domain this belongs to
    stakeholders: People involved
    criticality: One of: low, medium, high, critical
    due_at: Hard deadline (ISO datetime string)
    channel: Interaction channel
    context: Situational context
    parent_id: Parent actionable ID
    source: Where this came from
    metadata: Additional metadata
    proposed_deadline: Optional proposed deadline (ISO date, e.g. '2026-03-01')
    deadline_rationale: Why the deadline was proposed (e.g. 'sprint boundary')

get_actionable.description:
Get full details of an actionable including computed risk score.

Args:
    actionable_id: The actionable ID (e.g., 'act-a1b2c3d4')

update_actionable.description:
Update an existing actionable's fields.

Args:
    actionable_id: The actionable ID
    status: New status (open/in_progress/blocked/done/expired/cancelled)
    title: New title
    description: New description
    criticality: New criticality (low/medium/high/critical)
    due_at: New deadline (ISO datetime)
    add_risk_factor: Add a risk factor event description
    add_stakeholder: Add a stakeholder
    channel: Update interaction channel
    context: Update interaction context
    metadata: Merge additional metadata

list_actionables.description:
List actionables with optional filters, sorted by risk score.

Args:
    domain: Filter by domain
    status: Filter by status
    type: Filter by type
    include_risk: Include computed risk scores (default true)
    limit: Maximum results to return (default 50, max 100)

set_aow.description:
Set the Action Opportunity Window for an actionable.

Args:
    actionable_id: The actionable ID
    earliest: Can't act before this (ISO datetime)
    optimal_start: Best time to start (ISO datetime)
    optimal_end: Best time to finish (ISO datetime)
    latest: Hard deadline (ISO datetime)
    working_hours_start: Daily start time (HH:MM)
    working_hours_end: Daily end time (HH:MM)
    timezone: IANA timezone
    blocked_by: List of actionable IDs that must complete first

set_domain_policy.description:
Set access and retention policy for a memory domain.

Args:
    domain: The domain name
    allowed_agents: List of agent names that can access this domain
    confidentiality: standard/high/critical
    retention: permanent/session/ttl
    default_ttl: Default TTL in seconds
    description: Human-readable description

get_domain_policy.description:
Get the policy for a memory domain.

Args:
    domain: The domain name

post_to_slack.description:
Post a message to Slack via the CARLOS-OS bot.

Args:
    message: The message text to post (supports Slack mrkdwn).
    channel: Slack channel ID. Defaults to CARLOS_SLACK_CHANNEL.
    thread_ts: Optional thread timestamp to reply in a thread.

list_domains.description: List all known memory domains and their fact counts.
